question.text: What is the concept of 'Periodic Updation of KYC' and why is it important?

answer.text: Regularly refreshing customer identification data and risk profiles to ensure information remains current and accurate
Asking customers to submit new KYC documents every month
Updating KYC records only when the customer makes a complaint
Periodically deleting old KYC records to free up storage space

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Periodic KYC updation ensures that customer information remains current, allowing the institution to maintain accurate risk profiles. Changes in customer circumstances (address, occupation, income) can affect their risk rating and require updated due diligence. **The Trap:** KYC updation frequency depends on risk category — high-risk clients may need annual updates while low-risk clients may need updates every 10 years. It is not monthly. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A and Series VI test periodic KYC updation requirements and timelines. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Set up automated reminders for KYC updation based on client risk category — proactive outreach is better than reactive scrambling when regulators ask.
